extra.reasoning: rec_drug_b is set true only by rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) when hepatic_impairment is true; no rule ever sets rec_drug_b false, so rec_drug_b=true iff hepatic_impairment=true. rec_drug_a is set true only by rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50) when hepatic_impairment AND hyperkalemia AND low_ejection_fraction AND pregnancy AND elderly are all true. Crucially, the conjunction that enables rec_drug_a includes hepatic_impairment, which simultaneously fires rec_b_from_trigger. There is no priority conflict or false-setting rule to suppress either output (both are explicit set-true rules; the default only applies to unset outputs). Therefore any input satisfying the conjunction makes BOTH rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b true, violating the mutual-exclusion property. Concrete counterexample: hepatic_impairment=hyperkalemia=low_ejection_fraction=pregnancy=elderly=true (bradycardia=heart_failure=false). This yields rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true.
